label: false-dup
a: User profile data is cached in Redis with a 5-minute TTL and LRU eviction. Profiles are read on nearly every authenticated request, and profile updates invalidate the cache key immediately.
b: Real-time notifications use Redis pub/sub. When a notification event is generated, it is published to a user-specific channel (user:{id}:notifications). Connected clients subscribe to their channel and receive notifications with sub-100ms latency.
reviewer_notes: Both use Redis but for different patterns — A is caching (key/value with TTL); B is pub/sub (channels and subscriptions). Different Redis features, different data flows.
